# Learn Pretext

> An interactive learning site for @chenglou/pretext — a JavaScript library for multiline text measurement and layout without DOM reflow.

## About

Learn Pretext (learn-pretext.com) is an independent educational resource built by En Dash (endash.us). It teaches developers how to use the pretext library through interactive demos with annotated source code.

Pretext is a library by Cheng Lou (github.com/chenglou) that computes multiline text line breaks, heights, and widths using pure arithmetic — no DOM reads, no layout thrashing. It is ~300x faster than browser measurement.

This site is NOT an official project of Cheng Lou or the pretext library.

## Key pretext concepts

- `prepare(text, font)` — one-time measurement pass, caches glyph widths (~0.1ms)
- `layout(prepared, maxWidth, lineHeight)` — pure arithmetic layout, returns lineCount + height (~0.005ms)
- `prepareWithSegments()` — like prepare but exposes segment data (text, widths, break kinds)
- `layoutWithLines()` — returns array of LayoutLine objects with per-line text and width
- `layoutNextLine()` — iterator-style, one line at a time with variable maxWidth per line
- `walkLineRanges()` — callback per line with width data, no string allocation

## Site structure

20 pages across 5 learning tracks:

### Foundations (3 pages)
- Home — hero with live text reflow animation and performance counter
- Why Pretext — layout thrashing explanation, DOM vs pretext benchmark
- Getting Started — install, first example, interactive sandbox

### Core Patterns (5 pages)
- Accordion — height prediction with prepare() + layout()
- Chat Bubbles — shrink-wrap with walkLineRanges()
- Masonry — card grid with predicted heights
- Balanced Text — binary search for optimal width
- The Pipeline — prepare() internals visualization

### Advanced (5 pages)
- Rich API — three API paths compared side by side
- Editorial Layout — layoutNextLine() with draggable obstacles at 60fps
- Virtualized Lists — 10,000 items with pretext height prediction
- Canvas Rendering — multiline text on canvas with pan/zoom
- i18n Deep Dive — CJK, Arabic, Thai, Hindi, emoji handling

### Creative (2 pages)
- Kinetic Typography — wave text, scatter/gather animation
- ASCII Art — proportional ASCII rendering with pretext-measured widths

### Reference (5 pages)
- API Reference — all functions documented with interactive try-it blocks
- Performance Guide — benchmarks, caching, browser profiles
- Caveats & Recipes — system-ui bug, font matching, code patterns
- Accessibility — a11y patterns for pretext-powered UIs
- About — attribution and credits

## Tech stack

- Vanilla TypeScript + Vite (multi-page app)
- @chenglou/pretext for text measurement
- Shiki for code highlighting
- Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts
- GitHub Pages deployment

## Links

- Site: https://learn-pretext.com
- Source: https://github.com/en-dash-consulting/learn-pretext
- Pretext library: https://github.com/chenglou/pretext
- Built by: https://endash.us
